results.finalAnswer:
ENTRYPOINT: game/main.cpp
SUBSYSTEM: engine/core - engine lifecycle, ECS wiring, and gameplay-facing command/control flow start here
SUBSYSTEM: engine/voxel - world state, materials, chunk data, and voxel interactions are the core gameplay substrate
SUBSYSTEM: engine/rendering - gameplay changes need visible feedback here, especially camera/world presentation and debug visibility

ENTRYPOINT: game/main.cpp
SUBSYSTEM: engine/core - Engine orchestration, ECS wiring, and gameplay-facing control flow start here.
SUBSYSTEM: engine/voxel - World state, chunk/material logic, and voxel interactions live here.
SUBSYSTEM: engine/rendering - Gameplay changes must be visible and verified through the render pipeline.

ENTRYPOINT: game/main.cpp
SUBSYSTEM: engine/core - engine loop, ECS wiring, and gameplay-facing command/debug integration
SUBSYSTEM: engine/voxel - world state, materials, chunk logic, and most gameplay-environment interactions
SUBSYSTEM: engine/rendering - visual validation path for gameplay changes, including material/shader-side behavior

ENTRYPOINT: game/main.cpp
SUBSYSTEM: game/ecs/GameComponents.h - gameplay ECS schema; defines Health, Inventory, SkillSet, StatusEffects, MagicInventory, and other state every system shares.
SUBSYSTEM: game/systems/PlayerMovementSystem.cpp - player locomotion/collision/water/wind/fall-damage loop; central for anything that changes feel or traversal.
SUBSYSTEM: game/systems/VoxelInteractionSystem.cpp - mining/placing/melee core; ties input and camera to chunk edits, drops, inventory, and skill XP.

ENTRYPOINT: game/main.cpp
SUBSYSTEM: game/ecs/GameComponents.h - canonical gameplay state for player, skills, inventory, status effects, and voxel interaction
SUBSYSTEM: game/systems/VoxelInteractionSystem.cpp - main action loop for mining, building, melee combat, loot drops, and skill XP awards
SUBSYSTEM: game/systems/DeathSystem.cpp - core progression loop where death ripens skills, wipes temporary inventory, and respawns the player

ENTRYPOINT: game/main.cpp
SUBSYSTEM: engine/core/Engine_gameplay.cpp - Engine-side gameplay bridge: game state, save/load, pending actions, and callbacks that connect the engine to Flecs gameplay code.
SUBSYSTEM: game/ecs/GameComponents.h - Canonical gameplay data model: player health, inventory, skills, death, status effects, and magic are defined here for all systems to share.
SUBSYSTEM: game/systems/VoxelInteractionSystem.cpp - Highest-leverage player mechanic loop: mining, placing, melee, drops, durability, and skill XP all converge here.
